title: Bulk RNA Extraction from Mouse Striatum
author: sriparna.mukherjee

steps.title: Dissect the striatum from the mouse brain using clean instruments
steps.instruction: Dissect the striatum from the mouse brain using clean instruments.

steps.title: Immediately place each sample into a pre-labeled RNase-free microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Immediately place each sample into a pre-labeled RNase-free microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Either proceed directly to extraction or...
steps.instruction: Either proceed directly to extraction or snap freeze tissue and store at -80 °C until use.

steps.title: Transfer the sample to a RNeasy...
steps.instruction: Transfer the sample to a RNeasy spin column placed in a collection tube. ( 700 µL at a time)

steps.title: Wash the column using the kit-provided...
steps.instruction: Wash the column using the kit-provided wash buffers (RW1 and RPE) in the sequence and volume specified by the supplier.

steps.title: Centrifuge after each wash step as directed by the manufacturer
steps.instruction: Centrifuge after each wash step as directed by the manufacturer.

steps.title: Perform the final dry spin to...
steps.instruction: Perform the final dry spin to remove residual wash buffer and ethanol. Critical step: Residual ethanol can interfere with downstream RNA quantification, quality assessment, enzymatic reactions, and library preparation.

steps.title: Add the appropriate volume of Trizol to the striatal tissue
steps.instruction: Add the appropriate volume of Trizol to the striatal tissue. ( 300 µL of Trizol is used for the entire striatum isolated from both hemispheres of the brain)

steps.title: Homogenize the tissue thoroughly until the...
steps.instruction: Homogenize the tissue thoroughly until the lysate is uniform and no visible fragments remain. Critical step: Incomplete homogenization can reduce yield and clog the column. Brain tissue should be fully disrupted before proceeding.

steps.title: Add equal volume of 70% ethanol to the homogenized lysate
steps.instruction: Add equal volume of 70% ethanol to the homogenized lysate.

steps.title: Mix well by pipetting or gentle...
steps.instruction: Mix well by pipetting or gentle inversion until the solution is homogeneous.

steps.title: Centrifuge according to the kit's instructions...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge according to the kit's instructions to allow binding of the RNA to the membrane.

steps.title: If the full lysate volume exceeds...
steps.instruction: If the full lysate volume exceeds the column capacity, reload the remaining lysate and repeat until all material has passed through the column.

steps.title: Transfer the spin column to a clean RNase-free microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the spin column to a clean RNase-free microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add RNase-free water ( 30 µL...
steps.instruction: Add RNase-free water ( 30 µL ) directly onto the membrane.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge to elute RNA. Critical point
steps.instruction: Centrifuge to elute RNA. Critical point: Purified RNA may be stored temporarily on ice for immediate quality measurements or at -80°C for long-term storage.

steps.title: Measure RNA concentration using a Nanodrop
steps.instruction: Measure RNA concentration using a Nanodrop.

steps.title: Assess RNA quality and integrity using...
steps.instruction: Assess RNA quality and integrity using an Agilent BioAnalyzer or other similar instrument.

steps.title: RNA with a good RIN (RNA...
steps.instruction: RNA with a good RIN (RNA integrity number) can be used for RNA sequencing.
